description_long: This is a three-story corner pastry shop with a classic European architectural style, featuring a light-colored facade adorned with decorative window frames and cornices. The ground floor has large windows and an awning above the main entrance, suggesting a welcoming storefront. Outdoor seating is provided with small round tables and chairs, accompanied by potted plants and flower arrangements along the sidewalk. The upper floors have shuttered windows and subtle ornamental details, while the roof is bordered by a low balustrade. The building sits on a dark, angular base that mimics a city sidewalk and street corner. The overall appearance is elegant and inviting, suitable for a bustling urban neighborhood.
description: A three-story European-style pastry shop with decorative windows, an awning, and outdoor seating on a city corner. The facade is light-colored with classic architectural details and potted plants.
description_view_0: Front corner view showing the main entrance, awning, outdoor tables, and flower arrangements.
description_view_1: Alternate front angle highlighting the full width of the facade, more outdoor seating, and additional windows.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the plain wall with no windows or decorative features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, also showing a blank wall with no windows or details.
attribute_storefront_signage: None. There is no visible signage, text, or logo on the storefront or awning in any of the views.
attribute_displayed_pastries: None. No pastries are visible on display in the windows or outside the shop.
attribute_outdoor_seating: Outdoor seating is present, consisting of at least two small round tables with matching chairs arranged along the front and side of the shop. The seating is accompanied by potted plants and flowers, creating a cozy and inviting atmosphere.
attribute_window_displays: None. The windows are clear and do not contain any visible displays, decorations, or products.
attribute_architectural_style: The architectural style is reminiscent of late 19th to early 20th-century European urban buildings, with features such as decorative cornices, window shutters, a balustraded roofline, and a symmetrical facade. The style blends elements of Neoclassical and Parisian Haussmannian architecture.
